CCR Improvement Loop — metrics & trends

Aggregates committed run-*.json files. Trends are read from data/; null means "not measured" and renders as a gap, never zero. Low-confidence values are flagged lc.

Loading…
Repos:

Rate trends over time

One chart per rate. One line per repo. null renders as a gap, never zero.

Addressed rate by severity

Higher is better. One line per severity across the shown runs.

Of the human review asks at each severity, the share CCR also raised. Split by severity so critical misses aren't hidden by easy nit catches.

Bug-fix PR rate by repo (latest run)

Share of merged PRs classified as bug-fix in each repo's latest window. A proxy for escaped bugs (lower/stable is better), not proof.

CCR comment outcomes by severity

Pooled across the shown runs. Higher addressed is better.

Of the CCR comments at each severity, the share the author addressed, rejected, or ignored. A high ignored share on critical comments is the real problem to watch.

Addressed vs ignored by PR type

Pooled across the shown runs.

Where CCR's comments land (acted on) versus where they go unaddressed, split by PR type. Denominator-weighted across the window.

Human asks per PR by PR type

Pooled across the shown runs. An average count, not a ratio.

Average distinct substantive human review asks per PR, by PR type. Shows which kinds of PRs draw the most human review attention.

CCR catch rate by PR type

Pooled across the shown runs. Higher is better.

Of the substantive, diff-visible issues reviewers raised on CCR-reviewed PRs, the share CCR independently caught — split by PR type. Small samples per type; read as a rough profile, not a precise number.

Per-run headline metrics

n/a = not measured (null denominator). lc = low confidence (small sample).
